name: Texas
customIntro: Texas has no state income tax, but it makes up for it with a 6.25% state sales tax rate — and when you factor in local city and county additions, most Texans pay a combined rate between 8.25% and 8.25%, the statutory maximum.

taxFreeWeekends.name: Back-to-School Sales Tax Holiday
taxFreeWeekends.items: Clothing under $100, school supplies under $100, backpacks under $100

taxFreeWeekends.name: Emergency Preparation Supplies Holiday
taxFreeWeekends.items: Generators under $3,000, batteries, flashlights, first aid kits

taxFreeWeekends.name: Energy Star Products Holiday
taxFreeWeekends.items: Energy Star air conditioners, refrigerators, ceiling fans, washers/dryers under $2,000

faqs.question: What is the sales tax rate in Texas?
faqs.answer: Texas has a 6.25% state sales tax rate. Local cities, counties, and transit authorities can add up to 2% more, bringing the maximum combined rate to 8.25%. Most major Texas cities — including Houston, Dallas, San Antonio, and Austin — charge the full 8.25% combined rate.

faqs.question: Do you pay sales tax on a used car in Texas?
faqs.answer: Yes. Texas charges a 6.25% motor vehicle sales tax on used car purchases. This is collected at the time of title transfer. Private party sales are taxed on the higher of the purchase price or the vehicle's Standard Presumptive Value (SPV).

faqs.question: What items are exempt from sales tax in Texas?
faqs.answer: Texas exempts most groceries (unprepared food), prescription drugs, and certain agricultural items from sales tax. Prepared restaurant food, most clothing, electronics, and household goods are all taxable.

faqs.question: Is there sales tax on food in Texas?
faqs.answer: Most unprepared grocery food is exempt from Texas sales tax. However, prepared food sold at restaurants, fast food chains, or ready-to-eat from a deli counter is taxable at the full rate.

faqs.question: When is Texas tax-free weekend 2026?
faqs.answer: Texas has three tax-free weekends in 2026: the Back-to-School holiday on the first weekend of August, the Emergency Preparation Supplies holiday on the last weekend of April, and the Energy Star Products holiday on Memorial Day weekend.

name: Florida
customIntro: Calculating sales tax in the Sunshine State involves combining the 6% state rate with various local surtaxes — meaning your final rate depends heavily on which county you're shopping in, ranging from 6% to 7.5%.

taxFreeWeekends.name: Back-to-School Sales Tax Holiday
taxFreeWeekends.items: Clothing, footwear, wallets, and bags $100 or less; school supplies $50 or less per item; learning aids and jigsaw puzzles $30 or less. Excludes purchases at airports, theme parks, entertainment complexes, and public lodging establishments.

taxFreeWeekends.name: Disaster Preparedness Sales Tax Holiday
taxFreeWeekends.dates: Late May / Early June (2 weekends — check Florida DOR for exact 2026 dates)
taxFreeWeekends.items: Flashlights, batteries, generators under $3,000, tarps, radios, first aid kits

faqs.question: What is the sales tax rate in Florida?
faqs.answer: Florida's state sales tax rate is 6%. However, most counties add a local surtax of 0.5% to 1.5%, bringing the typical combined rate to 7%–7.5%. Hillsborough County (Tampa) has the highest combined rate at 7.5%.

faqs.question: Do you pay sales tax on a used car in Florida?
faqs.answer: Yes. Florida charges a 6% state sales tax on used vehicle purchases, plus any applicable county surtax. The county surtax on vehicles is capped at the first $5,000 of the purchase price, which can save buyers money on more expensive vehicles.

faqs.question: What is exempt from sales tax in Florida?
faqs.answer: Florida exempts most groceries, prescription drugs, and medical equipment from sales tax. Most clothing is taxable, unlike some other states. Agricultural supplies and certain manufacturing equipment also qualify for exemptions.

faqs.question: Is there sales tax on groceries in Florida?
faqs.answer: Most unprepared food sold in grocery stores is exempt from Florida sales tax. This includes most items you'd buy at a supermarket. Prepared food, restaurant meals, and some snack items are taxable.

faqs.question: When is Florida tax-free weekend 2026?
faqs.answer: Florida's Back-to-School Sales Tax Holiday runs the entire month of August 2026 — August 1 through August 31. During this period, clothing, footwear, wallets, and bags priced $100 or less are exempt, along with school supplies priced $50 or less per item and learning aids or jigsaw puzzles priced $30 or less. The exemption does not apply to purchases made at airports, theme parks, entertainment complexes, or public lodging establishments.

name: California
customIntro: California has the highest state sales tax rate in the nation at 7.25%, and with local district taxes layered on top, some areas like Los Angeles and Long Beach reach combined rates of 10.25% — something every shopper and business owner should factor into their budget.
formula: Price × 0.1025 = sales tax owed (Los Angeles example)

faqs.question: What is the sales tax rate in California?
faqs.answer: California's statewide base sales tax rate is 7.25% — the highest in the US. On top of this, local district taxes can add 0.125% to 3%, depending on where you shop. Los Angeles and several Bay Area cities reach combined rates of 10.25%.

faqs.question: Does California have a tax-free weekend?
faqs.answer: No. California does not have a sales tax holiday or tax-free weekend. All standard taxable purchases are subject to the full combined rate year-round.

faqs.question: What is exempt from sales tax in California?
faqs.answer: California exempts most unprepared food, prescription drugs, and certain medical devices. Unlike many states, California also partially exempts certain manufacturing and farming equipment. Prepared food, electronics, clothing, and most consumer goods are fully taxable.

faqs.question: Do you pay sales tax on a used car in California?
faqs.answer: Yes. California charges sales tax on used vehicle purchases. The rate is based on where you register the vehicle, not where you buy it. The combined rate in your county of registration applies, which can range from 7.25% to over 10%.

faqs.question: Why is California sales tax so high?
faqs.answer: California's high sales tax reflects its multi-layer tax structure: a 7.25% state base rate plus local district taxes added by cities, counties, and special districts for specific purposes like transportation or public safety. Voters in many jurisdictions have approved additional local measures over the years, which compound the state rate.

name: Ohio
customIntro: Ohio's sales tax structure combines a 5.75% state base rate with mandatory county-level additions, meaning the rate you pay depends entirely on where you make your purchase — ranging from 6.5% in some rural counties all the way to 8% in Cuyahoga County (Cleveland).
formula: Price × 0.075 = sales tax owed (Columbus / Franklin County example)

taxFreeWeekends.name: Sales Tax Holiday — Back-to-School
taxFreeWeekends.items: Clothing priced at $75 or less per item, school supplies $20 or less per item, school instructional materials $20 or less per item

faqs.question: What is the sales tax rate in Ohio?
faqs.answer: Ohio's state sales tax rate is 5.75%. Every county in Ohio adds an additional local tax, bringing the combined rate to between 6.5% and 8% depending on the county. Cuyahoga County (Cleveland) has the highest combined rate at 8%, while Franklin County (Columbus) and Hamilton County (Cincinnati) are both at 7.5%.

faqs.question: What is the sales tax in Columbus, Ohio?
faqs.answer: Columbus is located in Franklin County, which has a combined sales tax rate of 7.5% — the 5.75% state rate plus a 1.75% Franklin County addition. This applies to most retail purchases made within Columbus city limits.

faqs.question: Does Ohio have a sales tax holiday in 2026?
faqs.answer: Yes. Ohio holds an annual back-to-school sales tax holiday on the first Friday through Sunday of August. During this weekend, clothing items priced at $75 or less, school supplies priced at $20 or less per item, and school instructional materials priced at $20 or less per item are exempt from both state and local sales tax.

faqs.question: Is there sales tax on groceries in Ohio?
faqs.answer: Most unprepared food items are exempt from Ohio sales tax. This includes grocery staples bought at supermarkets. However, prepared food sold at restaurants, fast food chains, and ready-to-eat deli items are subject to the full combined sales tax rate.

faqs.question: Do you pay sales tax on a used car in Ohio?
faqs.answer: Yes. Ohio charges sales tax on used vehicle purchases at the combined rate for the county where the vehicle is titled. There is no cap on the taxable amount — the full purchase price is subject to tax. The tax is collected at the time of title transfer at the county BMV.

faqs.question: What is the sales tax rate in Cleveland, Ohio?
faqs.answer: Cleveland is in Cuyahoga County, which has the highest combined sales tax rate in Ohio at 8% — the 5.75% state rate plus a 2.25% Cuyahoga County addition. This applies to most taxable purchases made within the Cleveland metro area.

name: Arizona
customIntro: Arizona calls its sales tax a Transaction Privilege Tax (TPT) — a subtle but important distinction, because it is technically a tax on the seller's privilege of doing business, not on the buyer directly. In practice it works the same way, but the combined state and local rates vary significantly by city, from 5.6% in unincorporated areas to over 11% in some municipalities.

faqs.question: What is the sales tax rate in Arizona?
faqs.answer: Arizona's state Transaction Privilege Tax (TPT) rate is 5.6%. Cities and counties add their own rates on top, bringing most combined rates to between 7.6% and 11.1% depending on the city. Phoenix has a combined rate of 8.6%, while Sedona reaches 9.9%.

faqs.question: What is the sales tax rate in Phoenix, Arizona?
faqs.answer: Phoenix has a combined sales tax rate of 8.6%, made up of the 5.6% state TPT rate, a 0.7% Maricopa County rate, and a 2.3% City of Phoenix rate. This applies to most retail purchases made within Phoenix city limits.

faqs.question: Does Arizona have a sales tax holiday?
faqs.answer: No. Arizona does not currently have a sales tax holiday or tax-free weekend. All standard taxable purchases are subject to the full combined TPT rate year-round.

faqs.question: What is Arizona's Transaction Privilege Tax (TPT)?
faqs.answer: Arizona's Transaction Privilege Tax is the state's version of a sales tax. The key technical difference is that it is legally imposed on the seller for the privilege of doing business in Arizona, rather than on the buyer. In practice, sellers pass the cost to the consumer, so it functions identically to a conventional sales tax from the buyer's perspective.

faqs.question: Do you pay sales tax on a used car in Arizona?
faqs.answer: Yes. Arizona charges a vehicle license tax and sales tax on used car purchases. The sales tax rate is based on where you register the vehicle, not where you buy it. For most Phoenix-area buyers, the combined rate is 8.6% of the purchase price.

faqs.question: What is the sales tax in Tucson, Arizona?
faqs.answer: Tucson has a combined sales tax rate of 8.7%, consisting of the 5.6% state rate, a 0.5% Pima County rate, and a 2.6% City of Tucson rate. This applies to most taxable retail purchases made within Tucson city limits.

name: Pennsylvania
customIntro: Pennsylvania keeps its sales tax structure relatively simple compared to many states — a flat 6% statewide rate with only one local addition: Philadelphia adds 2% and Allegheny County (Pittsburgh) adds 1%, making those the only two jurisdictions in the state with a rate above 6%.
formula: Price × 0.06 = sales tax owed (most of PA) or Price × 0.08 = sales tax owed (Philadelphia)

faqs.question: What is the sales tax rate in Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Pennsylvania's statewide sales tax rate is 6%. Most counties have no additional local tax, so 6% applies across most of the state. The two exceptions are Philadelphia (8% combined) and Allegheny County including Pittsburgh (7% combined).

faqs.question: What is the sales tax rate in Philadelphia?
faqs.answer: Philadelphia has a combined sales tax rate of 8% — the standard 6% Pennsylvania state rate plus an additional 2% Philadelphia local tax. This applies to most taxable purchases made within Philadelphia city limits.

faqs.question: What is exempt from sales tax in Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Pennsylvania has notably broad sales tax exemptions. Most food (excluding restaurant meals and prepared food), clothing, and prescription drugs are exempt from sales tax. This makes Pennsylvania one of the more consumer-friendly states for everyday purchases, as clothing exemptions are relatively rare nationally.

faqs.question: Is clothing taxable in Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: No. Most clothing is exempt from Pennsylvania sales tax statewide, including in Philadelphia and Pittsburgh. This is one of Pennsylvania's most significant exemptions and applies to everyday apparel. Formal wear rentals and certain accessories may be taxable.

faqs.question: Do you pay sales tax on a used car in Pennsylvania?
faqs.answer: Yes. Pennsylvania charges 6% sales tax on used vehicle purchases statewide. Purchases made in Philadelphia are subject to the 8% combined rate. The tax is collected at the time of title transfer and is based on the higher of the purchase price or the vehicle's market value as determined by PennDOT.

faqs.question: Does Pennsylvania have a sales tax holiday?
faqs.answer: No. Pennsylvania does not have a general sales tax holiday. However, because clothing is permanently exempt from sales tax year-round, back-to-school shopping for apparel is always tax-free in Pennsylvania — making a dedicated holiday unnecessary for most shoppers.
